MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hewlett-Packard Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. The pleadings frame and limit the issues on a motion for summary judgment. (Weil and Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 10:17). Plaintiff's initial complaint is no longer valid because a demurrer was sustained to it. The Court has treated the unfiled first amended complaint as the operative complaint, but the only specific asbestos-containing product alleged in that complaint which plaintiff or his father was allegedly exposed to is a gas chromatograph. HP satisfied its burden of production by presenting evidence that plaintiff's father did not work with, or around others working with, a gas chromatograph during the employment of plaintiff's father at HP. Plaintiff failed to present any evidence creating a triable fact on that issue. While plaintiff's responses to HP's special interrogatories listed other products to which plaintiff's father may have been exposed, none of those other products are mentioned in the first amended complaint and plaintiff has not sought or been granted permission to file a further amended complaint. To the extent that plaintiff's opposition papers may be construed as seeking a continuance due to HP's failure to provide substantive responses to document requests, the continuance request is denied due to the fact that plaintiff has not acted in a timely fashion to seek substantive responses and the trial date is fast approaching. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not Reported. =(220/HEK)
